title: Genesis Vocabulary v1
description: Starter vocabulary practice pack — early Genesis language and concepts.

source_notes: Seeded from starter list: structure, saw, void, energy, spirit, alright, separated, everything, mixed, waters, gather, gathered, valid, vegetation.
Corrected 'vegatation' → 'vegetation' if ever typed that way in source.
Kept 'alright' and 'valid' as requested; swap to 'light' / 'divide' / 'firmament' in v2 if lesson should track KJV wording more tightly.

passage: Genesis means beginning or origin. In the beginning, the world lacked structure over deep waters — a void before separation. Spirit and energy are lesson words for God’s moving work: separated light from darkness, gathered waters, water and seas, vegetation on the land. God saw that the design was good. Words in this pack also practice informal discussion words like alright and valid, and big ideas like everything, mixed, and gathered.

rounds.title: Define: genesis
rounds.prompt: In this lesson, what does 'genesis' (or Genesis) mean?
rounds.rubric: A valid answer connects genesis to a beginning, start, origin, or the first part of creation.

rounds.title: Define: void
rounds.prompt: What does 'void' mean in this lesson?
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains that void means empty space, nothingness, or a place without form or contents yet.

rounds.title: Define: spirit
rounds.prompt: What does 'spirit' mean in this lesson?
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains spirit as a living presence, breath, wind, or unseen power moving over creation.

rounds.title: Use: separated
rounds.prompt: Use the word 'separated' in a sentence about creation.
rounds.rubric: A valid answer uses separated to show things being divided or set apart (light/dark, waters, etc.).

rounds.title: Define: waters
rounds.rubric: A valid answer connects waters to the deep, seas, or gathered bodies of water in the creation narrative.

rounds.title: Define: water
rounds.prompt: What does 'water' mean in the creation story? (How is it related to 'waters' or the deep?)
rounds.rubric: A valid answer names water as the liquid, seas, deep, or the stuff God gathers or separates in creation.

rounds.title: Define: gather
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains gather as bringing things together into one place.

rounds.title: Use: gathered
rounds.prompt: Use 'gathered' in a sentence about the waters or land in creation.
rounds.rubric: A valid answer uses gathered to show things being collected or brought together (waters, creatures, etc.).

rounds.title: Define: vegetation
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains vegetation as plant life — grass, herbs, trees, green growth.

rounds.title: Use: structure
rounds.prompt: Use the word 'structure' in a sentence about the world taking shape.
rounds.rubric: A valid answer uses structure to show order, arrangement, or form emerging in creation.

rounds.title: Define: mixed
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains mixed as combined or blended rather than fully separated.

rounds.title: Define: saw
rounds.prompt: In Bible-style language, what does it often mean when it says God 'saw' something?
rounds.rubric: A valid answer connects saw to noticing, observing, or approving what was made — not just eyesight in a cartoon sense.

rounds.title: Define: energy
rounds.prompt: What does 'energy' suggest in this lesson’s creation vocabulary?
rounds.rubric: A valid answer ties energy to power, life-force, movement, or God’s creative activity (student-level wording is fine).

rounds.title: Define: everything
rounds.prompt: What does 'everything' mean in the phrase 'over everything' or 'all creation'?
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains everything as all things, the whole creation, or the full set of what exists in context.

rounds.title: Define: alright
rounds.prompt: In everyday English, what does 'alright' usually mean? (This lesson uses it as a casual word — not the same as theological 'light'.)
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains alright as okay, acceptable, or good enough in casual speech.

rounds.title: Define: valid
rounds.prompt: What does 'valid' mean when we say an answer or reason is valid?
rounds.rubric: A valid answer explains valid as reasonable, well-supported, acceptable, or logically sound.
